title: pyrus aria var. longifolia
intro: Pyrus aria var. longifolia, also known as the Himalayan cherry or long-leaved mountain ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Himalayan region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant's extracts and preparations have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and fever, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Traditional herbal preparations of Pyrus aria var. longifolia include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often consumed as teas or used topically to support overall well-being.
